message:
fix: fiedler_vector raises on disconnected / small graphs (br-r37-c1-s22qo)

fnx.fiedler_vector silently returned a numerically-meaningless
eigenvector for the second-smallest Laplacian eigenvalue when the
input was disconnected. The second eigenvalue equals 0 for
disconnected graphs and the corresponding eigenspace is degenerate,
so the returned vector was junk (varied by float64 noise).

nx raises ``NetworkXError('graph is not connected.')`` for the same
inputs.

Repro:
    >>> g = fnx.Graph([(1, 2), (3, 4)])
    >>> fnx.fiedler_vector(g)
    array([-0., -0., -0.70710678, -0.70710678])
    >>> nx.fiedler_vector(nx.Graph([(1, 2), (3, 4)]))
    NetworkXError: graph is not connected.

Drop-in code that does ``pytest.raises(NetworkXError, match='graph
is not connected')`` would not trigger on fnx.

Sister spectral functions ``algebraic_connectivity`` and
``spectral_ordering`` already raised correctly on the same inputs
(the former was fixed by br-r37-c1-pb97z) — ``fiedler_vector`` was
the remaining outlier.

Fix
---
``python/franken_networkx/__init__.py:fiedler_vector``: add the same
two guards that ``algebraic_connectivity`` already carries:
- ``len(G) < 2`` → NetworkXError("graph has less than two nodes.")
- ``not is_connected(G)`` → NetworkXError("graph is not connected.")

Both raise BEFORE the dense ``np.linalg.eigh`` call so disconnected
inputs never reach the solver.

Tests
-----
New ``tests/python/test_fiedler_vector_disconnected_parity.py``:
- test_disconnected_two_components_raises
- test_disconnected_only_self_loops_raises
- test_single_node_raises_less_than_two_nodes
- test_empty_graph_raises_less_than_two_nodes
- test_connected_path_graph_unchanged_after_fix (regression guard
  with ~1e-6 tolerance — nx uses an iterative tracemin_pcg solver
  while fnx uses dense eigh, so the values agree up to absolute
  value but with iterative noise)
- test_connected_complete_graph_unchanged_after_fix
- test_disconnected_caught_by_nx_class (drop-in: ``except
  nx.NetworkXError`` catches the fnx exception)
- test_disconnected_raises_for_both_normalized_modes
  (parametrized over ``normalized=False``/``True``; both Laplacian
  modes go through the new guards)

Full sweep
----------
6691 passed, 0 failed (was 6682 before — +9 new regression cases).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"br-r37-c1-rxigp","title":"parity: Graph(numpy_array) / Graph(pandas_df) silently drops edges; nx auto-converts","description":"fnx.Graph(numpy.ndarray) and fnx.Graph(pandas.DataFrame) return empty graphs (no edges, no nodes from the matrix). nx.Graph(matrix) auto-detects the type and dispatches to from_numpy_array / from_pandas_adjacency, parsing the matrix as an adjacency matrix. Drop-in code that passes a numpy/pandas matrix to the constructor gets a wrong graph silently. Both fnx.from_numpy_array and fnx.from_pandas_adjacency exist and work; the constructor just doesn't dispatch to them. Fix: extend _init_absorbing_dict_of_dicts to detect numpy.ndarray / pandas.DataFrame inputs and dispatch through the appropriate from_* helper, then copy the result into self.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-26T08:58:56.019912981Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-26T09:04:43.299067209Z","closed_at":"2026-04-26T09:04:43.297031615Z","close_reason":"Fixed in commit f2364bc. Graph constructor now auto-dispatches numpy.ndarray to from_numpy_array and pandas.DataFrame to from_pandas_adjacency, matching nx's to_networkx_graph contract. 10 parity tests freeze the contract.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"br-r37-c1-rygbm","title":"parity: align default-arg sentinels (stoer_wagner heap, capacity_scaling heap, adjacency_data attrs, trophic_* weight)","description":"Several fnx wrappers have None defaults where upstream nx uses concrete sentinel values, so default-arg introspection diverges. (1) stoer_wagner(G, weight, heap) — nx default heap=BinaryHeap class, fnx is None. (2) capacity_scaling(G, demand, capacity, weight, heap) — same heap default mismatch. (3) adjacency_data/adjacency_graph attrs — nx default {id: id, key: key} dict, fnx is None (so nx.adjacency_data(G) and fnx.adjacency_data(G) emit different JSON). (4) trophic_levels/trophic_differences/trophic_incoherence_parameter weight — nx default weight=weight, fnx is None (silently ignores edge weights by default). Align defaults; for heap=, accept None and substitute BinaryHeap so the public introspectable default matches nx; for trophic_*, change default to weight. for adjacency_data, default to nx attrs dict.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-26T03:22:48.044744102Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-26T03:31:37.968373367Z","closed_at":"2026-04-26T03:31:37.965965689Z","close_reason":"Already fixed in commit 3400846 (re-closing after concurrent agent reverted status to in_progress). 12 parity tests in test_default_arg_sentinel_parity.py freeze the contract.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"br-r37-c1-s1gt6","title":"testing: parity smoke test for the 80 OrangeHorse wrappers (regression guard)","description":"OrangeHorse session shipped 13 beads adding ~80 nx-parity wrappers (TSP, approximation, bipartite, DAG, structuralholes, node_classification, RCM, random_sequence, tree.branchings, tree.mst, classes.filters, utils.heaps, frozen, coloring strategies, matching_dict_to_set, greedy_color callable fix). Add a parity smoke test (tests/python/test_orangehorse_parity_smoke.py) that for each of these symbols verifies (a) it is exported in fnx.__all__, (b) it is on fnx as an attribute, and (c) for callables, exercising it with a tiny canonical graph does not raise. This guards against future regressions when the underlying _call_..._for_parity helpers, __all__ ordering, or wrapper signatures drift.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-26T00:14:39.563161661Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-26T00:44:40.534764215Z","closed_at":"2026-04-26T00:44:40.532912847Z","close_reason":"Shipped in cf0ff0d: 170-case smoke test covering all 80 OrangeHorse wrappers. All pass in 0.50s. Discovered upstream nx hang on strategy_saturation_largest_first(G, {}) with empty colors — worked around by exercising via greedy_color end-to-end instead.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
+{"id":"br-r37-c1-s22qo","title":"fiedler_vector silently returns junk on disconnected graphs; nx raises NetworkXError","description":"fnx.fiedler_vector silently returns a numeric vector for disconnected graphs (with or without self-loops). nx raises NetworkXError('graph is not connected.').

Repro:
  >>> g = fnx.Graph([(1,2),(3,4)])
  >>> fnx.fiedler_vector(g)
  array([-0., -0., -0.70710678, -0.70710678])
  >>> nx.fiedler_vector(nx.Graph([(1,2),(3,4)]))
  NetworkXError: graph is not connected.

  >>> fnx.fiedler_vector(fnx.Graph([(1,1),(2,2)]))
  array([0., 1.])
  >>> nx.fiedler_vector(nx.Graph([(1,1),(2,2)]))
  NetworkXError: graph is not connected.

Drop-in code that does pytest.raises(NetworkXError, match='graph is not connected') won't trigger on fnx — the silent return value is mathematically meaningless (the Fiedler vector is the eigenvector for the second-smallest eigenvalue, and on disconnected graphs the second eigenvalue is 0 so the eigenvector is degenerate).

Sister functions algebraic_connectivity and spectral_ordering already raise correctly on disconnected inputs — only fiedler_vector was the outlier.

Fix: add is_connected(G) guard at the top of fnx.fiedler_vector raising NetworkXError with nx's exact wording.","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-27T06:44:00.075352321Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-27T06:44:30.006275510Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"br-r37-c1-saf8a","title":"intersection / difference / symmetric_difference node + edge order drift from nx","description":"fnx.intersection, .difference, .symmetric_difference use the Rust binding's traversal which produces different node and edge iteration order from nx (and different edge tuple direction).

Repro (intersection):
  fg edges = [('a','b'),('b','c'),('c','d')]; fg nodes after sync = [a,b,c,d,e]
  fh edges = [('a','b'),('c','d'),('d','e')]; fh nodes after sync = [a,b,c,d,e]
  fnx.intersection(fg, fh).nodes() -> ['a','b','c','d','e']
  nx.intersection(fg, fh).nodes()  -> ['b','d','c','e','a']
  fnx edges -> [('a','b'),('c','d')]
  nx edges  -> [('b','a'),('d','c')]   <- edge direction also flips

nx implements intersection via set intersection and add_nodes_from(set) / add_edges_from(set), which gives Python-hash-based (but stable, deterministic) order. The Rust binding traverses internal adjacency in a different order.

Drop-in code that iterated R.nodes() or R.edges() of an intersection result drifted. Fix: delegate intersection / difference / symmetric_difference to nx via _call_networkx_for_parity (mirrors articulation_points / cycle_basis / find_cycle pattern).","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-26T14:09:23.978754190Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-26T14:13:02.982493371Z","closed_at":"2026-04-26T14:13:02.980521026Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"br-r37-c1-sd104","title":"parity: write_graphml_xml/lxml missed infer_numeric_types kwarg","description":"fnx.write_graphml has infer_numeric_types but the two named-variant aliases write_graphml_xml and write_graphml_lxml omitted it from their signatures, so write_graphml_xml(G, path, infer_numeric_types=True) raised TypeError. nx exposes it on all three. Aligned both wrappers to forward through to write_graphml. 5 parity tests freeze the contract.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-26T03:51:01.274232337Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-26T03:51:43.837990653Z","closed_at":"2026-04-26T03:51:43.836131344Z","close_reason":"Fixed in commit 0c2b828. write_graphml_xml/lxml now expose infer_numeric_types in slot #5 matching nx, and forward all kwargs through to write_graphml. 5 parity tests freeze the contract.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"br-r37-c1-sf1ku","title":"parity: G.edges(data=True) returns list snapshot, nx returns live EdgeDataView","description":"fnx Graph.edges(data=...) wrapper materializes the result to a list (line 289 in __init__.py), breaking the view-liveness semantic that nx provides. Drop-in code that captures view = G.edges(data=True) and iterates after mutating G sees the pre-mutation snapshot on fnx but the live state on nx. Also type(view) is 'list' on fnx vs 'EdgeDataView' on nx — breaks isinstance checks. Same for G.edges(nbunch=...) and G.edges(data='attr'). Fix: return a thin _DeferredEdgeDataView wrapper that re-invokes the underlying call on each access (iter/len/contains/repr) so semantics match nx's live view, with class name aligned to nx's EdgeDataView.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-26T08:01:38.302877903Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-26T08:08:56.869602358Z","closed_at":"2026-04-26T08:08:56.867656020Z","close_reason":"Fixed in commit 0fe6aec. G.edges(data=...) and G.edges(nbunch=...) now return a live EdgeDataView wrapper that re-fetches on each access, matching nx's contract. 13 parity tests freeze the contract.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

@@ -12914,6 +12914,18 @@ def fiedler_vector(
 
     import numpy as np
 
+    # br-r37-c1-s22qo: nx raises NetworkXError on disconnected graphs
+    # ('graph is not connected.'); fnx's dense eigh solver silently
+    # produced a numerically-meaningless eigenvector for the
+    # second-smallest eigenvalue (which equals 0 for disconnected
+    # inputs and yields a degenerate basis). Match nx's contract.
+    # Sister functions algebraic_connectivity and spectral_ordering
+    # already raise correctly — fiedler_vector was the outlier.
+    if len(G) < 2:
+        raise NetworkXError("graph has less than two nodes.")
+    if not is_connected(G):
+        raise NetworkXError("graph is not connected.")
+
     if normalized:
         L = normalized_laplacian_matrix(G, weight=weight).toarray()
     else:

@@ -0,0 +1,119 @@
+"""Parity for fiedler_vector on disconnected and small graphs.
+
+Bead br-r37-c1-s22qo. ``fnx.fiedler_vector`` silently returned a
+numerically-meaningless eigenvector for the second-smallest
+eigenvalue when the input was disconnected. nx raises
+``NetworkXError('graph is not connected.')``.
+
+Sister spectral functions ``algebraic_connectivity`` and
+``spectral_ordering`` already raised correctly on the same inputs;
+``fiedler_vector`` was the outlier.
+"""
+
+from __future__ import annotations
+
+import pytest
+
+import franken_networkx as fnx
+
+try:
+    import networkx as nx
+    HAS_NX = True
+except ImportError:
+    HAS_NX = False
+
+needs_nx = pytest.mark.skipif(not HAS_NX, reason="networkx not installed")
+
+
+@needs_nx
+def test_disconnected_two_components_raises():
+    G = fnx.Graph([(1, 2), (3, 4)])
+    GX = nx.Graph([(1, 2), (3, 4)])
+    with pytest.raises(fnx.NetworkXError, match=r"^graph is not connected\.$"):
+        fnx.fiedler_vector(G)
+    with pytest.raises(nx.NetworkXError, match=r"^graph is not connected\.$"):
+        nx.fiedler_vector(GX)
+
+
+@needs_nx
+def test_disconnected_only_self_loops_raises():
+    G = fnx.Graph([(1, 1), (2, 2)])
+    GX = nx.Graph([(1, 1), (2, 2)])
+    with pytest.raises(fnx.NetworkXError, match=r"^graph is not connected\.$"):
+        fnx.fiedler_vector(G)
+    with pytest.raises(nx.NetworkXError, match=r"^graph is not connected\.$"):
+        nx.fiedler_vector(GX)
+
+
+@needs_nx
+def test_single_node_raises_less_than_two_nodes():
+    G = fnx.Graph()
+    G.add_node(0)
+    GX = nx.Graph()
+    GX.add_node(0)
+    with pytest.raises(fnx.NetworkXError, match=r"less than two nodes"):
+        fnx.fiedler_vector(G)
+    with pytest.raises(nx.NetworkXError, match=r"less than two nodes"):
+        nx.fiedler_vector(GX)
+
+
+@needs_nx
+def test_empty_graph_raises_less_than_two_nodes():
+    with pytest.raises(fnx.NetworkXError, match=r"less than two nodes"):
+        fnx.fiedler_vector(fnx.Graph())
+    with pytest.raises(nx.NetworkXError, match=r"less than two nodes"):
+        nx.fiedler_vector(nx.Graph())
+
+
+@needs_nx
+def test_connected_path_graph_unchanged_after_fix():
+    """Regression guard — the connected-graph happy path must continue
+    to yield the same Fiedler vector (up to sign and small numerical
+    noise) as nx."""
+    import numpy as np
+
+    G = fnx.path_graph(5)
+    GX = nx.path_graph(5)
+    f = fnx.fiedler_vector(G)
+    n = nx.fiedler_vector(GX)
+    # Sign of an eigenvector is arbitrary; compare absolute values.
+    # nx uses an iterative solver (tracemin_pcg) so its output has
+    # ~1e-8 noise relative to fnx's dense eigh.
+    assert np.allclose(np.abs(f), np.abs(n), atol=1e-6)
+
+
+@needs_nx
+def test_connected_complete_graph_unchanged_after_fix():
+    import numpy as np
+
+    G = fnx.complete_graph(4)
+    GX = nx.complete_graph(4)
+    f = fnx.fiedler_vector(G)
+    n = nx.fiedler_vector(GX)
+    assert f.shape == n.shape == (4,)
+
+
+@needs_nx
+def test_disconnected_caught_by_nx_class():
+    """Drop-in: fnx-raised NetworkXError must be catchable via
+    ``except nx.NetworkXError`` since fnx's exception hierarchy is
+    registered as a subclass of nx's."""
+    G = fnx.Graph([(1, 2), (3, 4)])
+    try:
+        fnx.fiedler_vector(G)
+    except nx.NetworkXError:
+        return
+    pytest.fail("fnx.fiedler_vector should raise NetworkXError on disconnected input")
+
+
+@needs_nx
+@pytest.mark.parametrize("normalized", [False, True])
+def test_disconnected_raises_for_both_normalized_modes(normalized):
+    """Both the regular and normalized-Laplacian paths must raise on
+    disconnected inputs."""
+    G = fnx.Graph([(1, 2), (3, 4)])
+    GX = nx.Graph([(1, 2), (3, 4)])
+    with pytest.raises(fnx.NetworkXError, match=r"^graph is not connected\.$"):
+        fnx.fiedler_vector(G, normalized=normalized)
+    with pytest.raises(nx.NetworkXError, match=r"^graph is not connected\.$"):
+        nx.fiedler_vector(GX, normalized=normalized)
